_readme: Scanning index for version-pinned tools that Trivy CANNOT see because they are
hand-unpacked / built from source (no package-manager identity). This file does
NOT drive any build - the authoritative pins still live in the 'source' file of
each entry. scan-pinned-tools.py mirrors them here only so their versions can be
queried against a CVE database (OSV / NVD).
Invariant (enforced by --verify): for every entry, 'match' must be found verbatim
in 'source'. If someone bumps the real pin without updating this file, verify fails.
Identity: give ONE of 'osv' {ecosystem,name} (preferred) or 'cpe' (for standalone
tools OSV doesn't index). 'covered_elsewhere' documents entries kept only for
completeness because another scanner already sees them.

tools.name: protoc (protobuf compiler)
tools.note: Unzipped to /usr/local -> invisible to Trivy. Its Go runtime/codegen identity (google.golang.org/protobuf v1.36.11) is already in go.mod and scanned; this catches compiler-specific advisories.

tools.name: pandoc
tools.covered_elsewhere: Installed via 'dpkg -i' -> also visible in the man image's dpkg DB when Trivy scans that image. Kept here as a cross-check.

tools.name: clang / llvm
tools.covered_elsewhere: Installed via apk/apt -> visible as OS packages in the tracee-make images. Kept here as a cross-check.
tools.note: NVD product for the compiler is llvm:clang; LLVM library-level advisories may also appear under cpe:2.3:a:llvm:llvm.

tools.name: go toolchain
tools.covered_elsewhere: govulncheck (pr.yaml) already covers Go stdlib with reachability. Kept as a belt-and-suspenders example of an OSV-backed entry.

tools.name: govulncheck (dev tool)
tools.note: Pinned by commit; the semver in the trailing comment (v1.1.4) is what OSV matches.

tools.name: bpftool (from btfhub)
tools.note: Built from source via btfhub bpftool.sh with no version pin and no advisory-backed identity. No scanner can match it. Track via upstream libbpf/bpftool GitHub Security Advisories instead.
